Now there's no doors, just a flashlight and an empty mask, and the creepy music box keeps winding down.
It's exactly the same game, but now you can physically see the imposter aggressively pointing at you in a meeting.
I spawned a bunch of ragdolls, attached thrusters to a bathtub, and flew into the sky until the server crashed.
I shot a laser out of my eye at a giant pile of flesh. I have over 500 hours in this game.
A glowing white creature jumps around beautifully painted trees while an emotionally devastating soundtrack plays.
I have been waiting for this game to release for so long I am starting to believe it was just a collective hallucination.
The NPCs look like potatoes and talk to each other about mudcrabs in completely unnatural ways.
I drove a clumsy six-wheeled rover over jagged mountains on an empty planet for an hour just to find a piece of scrap metal.
I escaped a vault just to walk into a wasteland, nuke an entire town for a handful of bottle caps, and get attacked by giant mutated flies.
A big ape and a little ape ride minecarts and jump on crocodiles to get their bananas back.
A tennis game where your opponent can literally break your racket and win by default. Very fair sport.
I had to clean up a tropical island covered in graffiti using a talking water backpack.
A highly complex strategy game where I am yelled at in Russian for not buying the right items.
I finally got to play as a famous Jedi, but I immediately ran into a room full of grenades and died.
Running away from terrifying psychopaths in a creepy testing facility with friends who constantly leave me behind to save themselves.
